_risk_level_doc: Two files modified (settings.json, hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh) plus one created (hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py). settings.json is infrastructure-critical but the change is additive (new PreToolUse entry); the new hook is non-blocking on error paths. risk=medium.

requirement.clarified: Implement arch-6 (gitignore preflight gate as new PreToolUse hook) and arch-3 (ENOSPC fallback in L1.5 counter write) from spec-20260520-221059 priority order. K1 directive reversed by explicit user authorization. arch-7 phase 2 excluded (mandatory dedicated cycle). Layer A R1 satisfied by arch-6.
requirement.what: New hook hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py that blocks Agent dispatches when declared deliverables are gitignored; ENOSPC-safe counter write in hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh
requirement.why: arch-6 closes the gap that caused docs/reference/tmp-cleanup-convention.md to pass QA but not ship via git (gitignore match invisible to dev+QA pipeline). arch-3 prevents the L1.5 rate-limit counter from hanging or misfiring when /tmp is under genuine ENOSPC pressure.

arch-3: ENOSPC fallback for both counter-write lines in userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh flock block
excluded: arch-7 phase 2 (score CAS) — mandatory dedicated cycle
R3, R4, R5b, R5c, R5d, W1-W5

success_criteria: hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py exists, is executable, is registered in settings.json PreToolUse
Hook exits non-zero when a declared path is gitignored without gitignore_waiver
Hook exits 0 (no-op) when dev-report absent or no dev-report path in prompt
hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh ENOSPC failure at counter write exits 0 (skip, not emit)

DO NOT modify frozen spec docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-044700.md

arch-7 phase 2 must NOT be bundled into this cycle

out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: agents/dev.md:495 — files_created derivation does not surface ignored untracked files (Codex finding 4; known limitation of arch-6 scope)

out_of_scope_observations.observation: files_created derivation uses git ls-files --others --exclude-standard, which excludes ignored untracked files. An ignored deliverable omitted from the dev-report entirely is invisible to arch-6. Closing this gap requires an arch-6 phase 2 item (add ignored-untracked scan to dev derivation rules).

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Deliverables matched by .gitignore passed QA and commit without detection; the L1.5 counter write fails silently under ENOSPC causing emit-on-every-prompt.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: (arch-6) No automated gate exists to check dev_report.files_modified/files_created against .gitignore at cycle boundary. (arch-3) echo/printf redirect to $COUNTER_FILE has no failure branch; under ENOSPC the write fails but decision subshell falls through to printf 'emit'.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — these are gaps (missing features), not regressions introduced by a specific commit
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The original dev+QA pipeline was designed to verify behavior, not shippability via git. ENOSPC handling was not in scope when L1.5 was first implemented (cycle 161035).
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Gitignored deliverables appear to ship (QA passes, commit proceeds) but are absent from fresh clones. ENOSPC counter failure causes rate-limit bypass: all subsequent prompts emit warnings without incrementing the counter.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Gap predates this spec; surfaced by meta-assessment of cycle 161035 (spec Layer A, R1 and R5a).
observations: hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh:118 read — bare echo $((n + 1)) > COUNTER_FILE with no error check (inferred from file read)
hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh:115 read — printf '%s\n' $n > COUNTER_FILE with no error check (inferred from file read)
.gitignore read — docs/* blanket rule confirmed present (inferred from file read)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — ENOSPC path not runtime-observed; gitignore gate absence not observed via running cycle)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Source code read at hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh lines 112-122 confirms the flock block structure and the bare redirect at line 118. .gitignore read confirms docs/* rule. No runtime observation available (cannot manufacture ENOSPC in analysis phase).
root_cause_analysis.located_source: hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh:118 (arch-3 target); hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py (arch-6 target — does not exist yet)

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: Not applicable — these are missing-feature gaps, not regressions introduced by a specific commit

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Guard against flock timeout (contention); exits subshell with skip if flock cannot be acquired within 1 second
Rate-limit guard — prevents more than RATE_LIMIT=3 emissions per session

Changes are to PreToolUse hooks and a UserPromptSubmit hook only.

reference_source.description: Source code read in this session: hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh lines 100-147 and settings.json PreToolUse block. Codex gpt-5.5 adversarial review (session 019e5b01) provided 9 findings; 6 applied, 3 logged as observations.

attempts.proposed_solution: arch-1/arch-5/arch-7-phase-1/arch-8 — gitignore exceptions, install mirror, uncapped_delta, lifecycle.jsonl tracking
attempts.outcome: All 5 ACs passed. arch-6 and arch-3 were explicitly deferred.

novelty_check.rationale: Prior cycle fixed static config (gitignore rules); this cycle adds a runtime enforcement hook (L5 new gate) and a logic error-path fix (L4). Net-new changes with no repetition.

context.codebase_state: 22 dirty files in working tree from prior concurrent cycles; HEAD on master. logs/lifecycle.jsonl tracked. !docs/reference/ negation present in .gitignore. scores at dev:100, ba:100, qa:14.
context.recent_commits: af96c61 (M1-M4 cycle 20260522-000000), 5771880 (spec committed), then auto-bulk commits on master

dependencies.git: required for git check-ignore in arch-6 hook

development_approach.strategy: Create hooks/pretool-gitignore-preflight.py with proper no-op conditions (missing dev-report path in prompt; dev-report absent on disk; gitignore_waiver present). Register in settings.json PreToolUse. Patch hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh lines ~115 and ~118 with failure-aware write form. Write pytest test suite for all 8 ACs.
development_approach.validation_approach: pytest for AC1a, AC1b, AC2, AC3, AC4a, AC4b, AC5, AC6, AC7, AC7b, AC8, AC9, AC10, AC11. AC1a, AC1b, AC2, AC3, AC4a, AC4b, AC5, AC6, AC10, AC11: parametrized tests constructing mock dev-reports and calling the hook as subprocess. AC7: EISDIR harness — COUNTER_FILE pre-created as directory; no cat shim so n defaults to 0 (increment branch); df shim emits >75%; asserts exit 0 with empty stdout and stderr. AC7b: EISDIR harness — COUNTER_FILE pre-created as directory; cat shim emits '3' (RATE_LIMIT) so mtime-refresh branch fires; df shim emits >75%; asserts exit 0 with empty stdout and stderr (tests the saturated branch write failure path). AC8, AC9: source grep + bash -n syntax check.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex gpt-5.5 (session 019e5b01-9c62-7ff1-9d08-a9e7465f45d8) returned 9 findings: 3 BLOCKERs, 3 MAJORs, 3 OBSERVATION_ONLY. Main issues: hook needs deterministic dev-report target (parse from prompt, not glob); must no-op before dev-report exists; must use git check-ignore --quiet per-path (not raw -v output due to negation false positives); echo...||true is insufficient for ENOSPC (printf 'emit' executes regardless); lifecycle logging risks CAS scope creep; scope is right-sized if arch-3 stays surgical.

findings.issue: BLOCKER: arch-6 hook needs a deterministic dev-report target — cannot glob latest report from many stale ones
findings.proposed_fix: Register under matcher:Agent and parse dev-report path from the prompt JSON string via regex r'docs/dev/dev-report-[A-Za-z0-9._-]+\.json'. No-op if no match.
findings.rationale: Globbing latest dev-report-*.json is ambiguous in this repo with 20+ stale reports. Prompt-parsing is deterministic and exactly scoped.

findings.issue: BLOCKER: arch-6 must no-op before dev-report exists on disk
findings.proposed_fix: Exit 0 when dev-report path (extracted from prompt) does not exist on disk
findings.rationale: Pre-dev Agent dispatches would be blocked if the hook errored on missing file.

findings.issue: BLOCKER: use git check-ignore --no-index --quiet per-path (exit-code based), not raw -v output
findings.proposed_fix: Per-path subprocess.run(['git','check-ignore','--no-index','--quiet','--',path]); exit 0 = ignored, exit 1 = not ignored
findings.rationale: .gitignore has negations (!docs/reference/, !logs/lifecycle.jsonl); raw -v output mentions negation rules and produces false positives. Exit-code approach resolves the full negation chain.

findings.issue: MAJOR: declared-path-only arch-6 can miss ignored deliverables omitted from files_created under current dev derivation
findings.proposed_fix: Explicitly narrow arch-6 to declared paths only; document the known gap
findings.rationale: Expanding to scan all untracked files would require changing agents/dev.md derivation — out of scope for this cycle. Known gap documented in out_of_scope_observations.

findings.issue: MAJOR: arch-3 echo...||true insufficient — printf 'emit' executes regardless of write failure
findings.proposed_fix: if ! printf '%s\n' "$((n + 1))" > "$COUNTER_FILE"; then printf 'skip'; exit 0; fi; printf 'emit'
findings.rationale: Codex correctly identified that || true silences the error but does not change control flow — the subshell still falls through to printf 'emit'. The if ! form is required.

findings.issue: MAJOR: optional lifecycle logging in arch-3 risks scope creep into arch-7/R9 territory
findings.proposed_fix: Omit lifecycle logging for cycle 3 entirely
findings.rationale: logs/lifecycle.jsonl is tracked; writing from a UserPromptSubmit hook without the CAS architecture is a footgun. Omitted per spec Section 8(d) philosophy.

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY: arch-6 + arch-3 is right-sized only if arch-3 stays surgical (no timeout/cleanup/df/du changes)
findings.rationale: Scope constraints already enforce this; logged for QA awareness.

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY: PreToolUse hook is better than commands/dev.md prose step for arch-6
findings.rationale: Already using hook approach; confirmed correct.

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY: scope-creep risks (R2, R5b/R5c/R5d, R3/R4, arch-7-phase-2, broad Layer A)
findings.rationale: All enumerated items are in Won't Have; logged for QA awareness.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 3 BLOCKERs applied (deterministic path extraction, no-op before dev-report exists, git check-ignore --quiet). All 3 MAJORs applied (narrow to declared paths, if! form for ENOSPC, no lifecycle logging). 3 OBSERVATION_ONLY logged.
